mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer confidence and disposable income, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through higher expected corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09 payroll level 139,435k indicates ongoing expansion
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with steady labor market gains
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce higher average 20-day returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% after a prolonged near-zero period, it signals the end of emergency monetary accommodation and triggers equity repricing downward as discount rates increase and forward guidance becomes restrictive, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5% after 36+ months below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09 FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates continued accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in housing starts and retail sales signals broad-based demand strength and consumer willingness to commit to durable purchases, which correlates with positive equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series accelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase > 2% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08 HOUST 956k and RSAFS 442.7B indicate demand resilience
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint acceleration months show no statistically significant difference in 20-day returns versus baseline (p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines > 1% month-over-month signal supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08 INDPRO 104.12 shows recent production levels
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises > 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:2014-09 UNRATE 5.9% indicates labor market tightening
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, it signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression, which supports equity valuations through negative real rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08 CPIAUCSL 237.43 and 2014-09 FEDFUNDS 0.09% indicate accommodative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Joint condition produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in accommodative-inflation regimes show no significant outperformance versus baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerates below 2.0% annualized in consecutive quarters, signaling late-cycle slowdown and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Consecutive sub-2% growth quarters produce positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while payroll growth remains above 100,000 persons monthly, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports profit margins and productivity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases > 0.5% MoM AND PAYEMS monthly change > 100,000 persons in same month
evidence_refs: fact:2014-08 INDPRO 104.12 and 2014-09 PAYEMS 139,435k show supply-labor coordination
failure_condition: Joint acceleration signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or synchronized months show no statistically significant 20-day return advantage versus baseline (p > 0.08).
